observations: SCOPE OF THIS PASS: a full round-2 re-review (not a metadata-only delta), since revision 2 adds three new operative verification entries (6, 8, 11) plus HEAD-relative guard/version-check simplifications, not merely bookkeeping. All three of this evaluator's own round-1 findings (.agent/qa/039-contract-review.json issues[0..2]) were independently re-probed from scratch against the live repository and the generator's own new entries, not trusted from the generator's dry_run_proof or approvals.round1_rejection.fixed_in narrative.

SCHEMA VALIDITY AND ARRAY LENGTH: `jsonschema.validate` re-run against the live revision-2 contract and schema/contract.schema.json: exits 0, schema-valid. Parsed the live 'verification' array programmatically: exactly 31 entries, matching evidence_convention's own stated count (28 -> 31, +3 new entries). No off-by-one.

KILL-SHOT 1 RE-VERIFICATION (new entry 6, evidence-item path pattern, closes issues[1]): rebuilt this evaluator's own STRICT scratch schema (schema_design unchanged since revision 0/1) and reran entry 6's exact, live command in a disposable minitree: PASSES (both bad-prefix probes -- '/etc/passwd' and a relative path missing the '.agent/' prefix -- correctly rejected; a conforming path validates). Then built the exact GAP variant from the round-1 review (evidence_item.path's 'pattern' constraint dropped entirely, minLength retained): reran entry 6's identical command against it -- FAILS ('abs unexpectedly validated'), reproducing this evaluator's own round-1 kill shot precisely and confirming the fix is genuinely load-bearing, not merely present.

KILL-SHOT 2 RE-VERIFICATION (new entry 8, minLength on 5 required scalar fields, closes issues[0]): reran entry 8's exact command against the STRICT scratch schema -- PASSES. Built the exact GAP variant from round 1 (minLength dropped from id/severity/impact/owner/closure_criteria, fields remain 'required'): reran entry 8's identical command against it -- FAILS, reporting all five field names in the assertion's failures list ('id','severity','impact','owner','closure_criteria'), exactly reproducing this evaluator's own round-1 kill shot and confirming the fix's per-key loop structure (mirroring the pre-existing entry 7's absence-loop) correctly catches every one of the five fields, not just a subset.

KILL-SHOT 3 RE-VERIFICATION (new entry 11, maturity exact-vocabulary enforcement, closes issues[2]): reran entry 11's exact command against the STRICT scratch schema -- PASSES (bogus 'Battle-Tested' rejected; all ten real protocol-Sec-4 level names individually validate; omitting maturity entirely also validates, correctly proving optionality is preserved). Built the exact GAP variant from round 1 (maturity relaxed to a bare untyped string, no enum): reran entry 11's identical command against it -- FAILS ('bogus maturity value unexpectedly validated'), reproducing this evaluator's own round-1 kill shot and confirming the fix.

ALL THREE ROUND-1 FINDINGS ARE THEREFORE GENUINELY CLOSED, PROVEN IN BOTH DIRECTIONS (GAP variant fails the new entry; STRICT/actual design passes it), not merely asserted closed by the generator's own narrative.

SOUNDNESS CARRIED FORWARD, INDEPENDENTLY RE-PROBED (not assumed from the round-1 review's prior findings): re-ran all 14 content-anchored, non-git-dependent entries (1, 3-15) against the disposable STRICT minitree -- all 14 PASS. Re-ran the LOOSE closure-conditional variant (allOf deleted, closure_evidence relaxed to untyped array) against entries 9, 10, 12, 13, 14 (renumbered from rev1's 7, 8, 10, 11, 12): entries 10 and 14 correctly FAIL under LOOSE (content-completeness probe and the named resolved-without-closure-evidence fixture check), entries 9, 12, 13 pass under LOOSE too but that is expected -- they prove narrower, different properties (open-state non-over-constraint, both-positives, naming non-collision), not the closure conditional's strictness. The 8-required-key omission loop (entry 7, renumbered from rev1's entry 6) independently re-run against STRICT: still PASSES, all 8 keys still load-bearing for absence.

DUAL-SURFACE NIZAM.json PAIR RE-VERIFIED AGAINST THE POST-037-COMMIT REAL NIZAM.json (entries 18+19, renumbered from rev1's 15+16): built three fresh disposable scratch NIZAM.json variants directly from the CURRENT, live, post-8c7c3ee NIZAM.json (which already carries feature 037's own dual-surface-compliant ecosystem_baseline.schema.json entries in both surfaces) -- top-level-'schemas'-only, module-'key_documents'-only, and fully dual-compliant. Ran entries 18 and 19's exact, live command bodies against all three: top-level-only -> entry18 FAILS / entry19 PASSES; module-only -> entry18 PASSES / entry19 FAILS; dual-compliant -> both PASS. The pair still fully and uniquely distinguishes all three cases against the new, larger real NIZAM.json baseline; the round-0 gap remains closed.

SEVERITY-NOT-AN-ENUM AND CONFIDENCE-ENUM DESIGN DECISIONS: design_notes.severity_and_status_scope and schema_design are byte-identical to revision 1 for these two decisions (no textual change in revision 2); this evaluator's round-1 verbatim confirmation against ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md Sec 1 and Sec 5 therefore still holds and was not re-derived from scratch a second time, consistent with house convention for unchanged claims already independently verified.

GUARD / VERSION-CHECK SIMPLIFICATIONS RE-VERIFIED LIVE AGAINST THE REAL REPOSITORY AT HEAD 8c7c3ee (feature 037 now fully committed: schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json and its 3 fixtures tracked, NIZAM.json's top-level 'schemas' array now has 13 entries including it, schema/README.md frontmatter at 0.6.0): re-ran ALL git/NIZAM/README-dependent entries (2, 16-31) live and read-only against the real repository. Results exactly match the expected pre-implementation state the contract's own design_notes claims: entries 2, 17, 21, 23, 24, 25, 26, 27, 28, 29, 30, 31 PASS; entries 16, 18, 19, 20, 22 correctly FAIL (schema/README.md and NIZAM.json have not yet been edited by an unimplemented 039, and the working-tree schema/README.md version (0.6.0) is not yet a strict increase over HEAD's own 0.6.0 -- exactly what design_notes.version_robustness predicts: 'the check correctly FAILS today'). The simplified entry 27 (schema/*.json non-goal, no longer excluding schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json) and entry 31 (scope guard, no longer tolerating ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md, schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json, or its 3 fixtures) both PASS live in their simplified form, confirming the simplification is correct now that 037 is committed history rather than concurrent uncommitted state.

FRESH-DEFECT SWEEP ON THE THREE NEW ENTRIES: entry 8's per-key loop collects failures into a list and asserts non-empty at the end (mirrors entry 7's already-vetted pattern; no early-exit masking, all 5 fields independently visible on failure, confirmed by the GAP-variant re-run above reporting all 5 field names). Entry 11's omission check (`jsonschema.validate(base, s)` with no 'maturity' key) correctly proves optionality is preserved, not merely that the enum rejects bad values. Entry 6's SystemExit-inside-try structure was traced by hand and confirmed to propagate correctly to a non-zero exit when a bad path unexpectedly validates (not silently swallowed by the adjacent `except jsonschema.ValidationError` clause, which only catches the validate() call's own exception, not the deliberately-raised SystemExit) -- confirmed empirically by the GAP-variant re-run correctly failing with the exact SystemExit message.

ONE NEW, NARROWER, NON-BLOCKING RESIDUAL FOUND IN ENTRY 6 (probed adversarially per this round's mandate to hunt for a fresh defect class in the new entries, not treated as blocking at this final revision round): entry 6's two probe paths ('/etc/passwd' and 'evidence/e/1.txt') both entirely LACK the substring '.agent/evidence/', so entry 6 does not by itself distinguish a correctly-anchored pattern ('^\.agent/evidence/') from an UN-ANCHORED, substring-only variant (e.g. pattern '\.agent/evidence/' with no leading '^'). Built this specific UN-ANCHORED variant and reran entry 6's exact command against it: PASSES (both existing bad-prefix probes are still caught, since neither contains the substring at all), yet a direct probe not in the suite -- an evidence path 'foo/.agent/evidence/bar.txt', which contains the substring but not as a true string-start prefix -- VALIDATES under the un-anchored variant (should be rejected) and is correctly rejected only under the properly-anchored STRICT schema. This is a narrower, secondary-order gap than the round-1 findings: it does not represent a completely-missing constraint (the un-anchored variant still meaningfully constrains paths and still catches both of the concrete negative probes this contract actually tests), only a specific common regex-authoring mistake (a dropped '^' anchor) that entry 6 would not itself catch. Given this is the contract's final permitted revision round, and Mode B (post-code implementation review) independently compares the shipped schema's literal text against design_notes.schema_design's explicit, unchanged, and never-disputed declaration of the exact pattern string 'pattern "^\\.agent/evidence/"' -- providing a second, structural line of defense against exactly this defect class -- this observation is recorded as non-blocking. Recommendation for Mode B: explicitly confirm the shipped schema/engineering_finding.schema.json's evidence_item.path pattern includes the leading '^' anchor verbatim, not merely that a 'pattern' key is present.

CONCURRENCY / PROBE-ISOLATION: confirmed live throughout this review that HEAD is 8c7c3ee (feature 037 fully committed: 'feat(005): ecosystem baseline schema + fixtures (feature 037)'), feature 039 remains 'proposed' with its own untracked .agent/contracts/039.json, and feature 040 has a fresh untracked .agent/contracts/040.json (drafting) -- tolerated, not opened or touched, per the probe-isolation instruction. `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all` before and after this review's probes shows only 039's own untracked contract/review artifacts, 040's own untracked contract, and the pre-existing v0.6.0-release-notes.md; `git diff HEAD --stat` shows no tracked-file changes. All scratch schemas, fixtures, and disposable NIZAM.json variants used in this round-2 review live only under the session scratchpad directory, outside the repository tree. Nothing under schema/, ecosystem/, tools/, docs/, or NIZAM.json in the real repository, and no other feature's .agent/contracts or .agent/qa artifact, was created, edited, or deleted at any point in this review. No edit was made to .agent/run_state.json.

final_verdict.summary: APPROVED at revision 2, fit for implementation as-is. All three of this evaluator's own round-1 findings (.agent/qa/039-contract-review.json) are independently re-probed and confirmed genuinely closed, not merely asserted closed: the three new verification entries (6 -- evidence path pattern, 8 -- minLength on five required scalar fields, 11 -- maturity exact-vocabulary enforcement) each PASS against the contract's real, unmodified STRICT schema_design and each correctly FAIL against this evaluator's own exact round-1 GAP variants (pattern dropped, minLength dropped, maturity enum dropped respectively) -- a live, both-directions kill-shot reproduction, not a trust of the generator's narrative. Every mechanism confirmed sound in round 1 remains sound and was independently re-verified against the live repository at the new HEAD (8c7c3ee, feature 037 now fully committed): the loose-vs-strict closure conditional (entries 10/14 remain load-bearing against a fresh LOOSE scratch variant), the 8-required-key absence loop (entry 7, still catches all 8 keys), the confidence enum (unchanged, still closed to exactly three values), the severity-not-an-enum design decision (unchanged design text, previously verbatim-confirmed against ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md Sec 1), the dual-surface NIZAM.json pair (entries 18/19, re-verified against three fresh scratch variants built from the CURRENT, larger, post-037-commit real NIZAM.json -- still fully and uniquely distinguishing all three single-surface/dual cases), and the fixture-naming/glob partition (still non-colliding). The HEAD-relative guard and version-check simplifications (dropping now-unnecessary defensive tolerances for feature 037's and 036's since-committed paths) were re-run live, read-only, against the real repository at HEAD 8c7c3ee: every entry behaves exactly as design_notes predicts for the correct pre-implementation state (entries 16/18/19/20/22 correctly FAIL today because 039 has not yet implemented; entries 2/17/21/23-31 correctly PASS). One narrower, non-blocking residual was found by adversarially probing the three new entries for a fresh defect class: entry 6's two bad-path probes both entirely lack the '.agent/evidence/' substring, so entry 6 alone would not distinguish a correctly-anchored path pattern from an un-anchored, substring-only variant (which would wrongly accept a nested, non-prefix path such as 'foo/.agent/evidence/bar.txt'). This is a materially narrower gap than the three round-1 findings -- it does not represent a completely-missing constraint, only a specific regex-anchoring mistake -- and is mitigated by Mode B's independent comparison of the shipped schema's literal pattern text against design_notes.schema_design's explicit, unchanged '^\.agent/evidence/' declaration. Recorded as a non-blocking observation with a concrete recommendation for Mode B, not as a rejection reason, given this is the contract's final permitted revision round and no completely-unguarded acceptance-relevant claim remains. Fit for implementation as-is.
